description_long: This harp features a striking lyre-shaped frame with two gracefully curved arms rising from a robust rectangular base. The upper section is adorned with a set of six visible strings, each extending vertically from the base to the top arch, suggesting a classical design. The base is solid and blocky, providing stability, and the instrument sits on four sturdy legs, two of which are more pronounced and rounded at the front. The keyboard visible in the front view indicates this is a rare hybrid instrument, possibly a lyre-piano, blending string and keyboard elements. The surface appears smooth and hard, likely constructed from polished wood with metallic strings. There are no visible decorative carvings or inlays, giving the instrument a clean, elegant silhouette.
description: A lyre-shaped harp with a solid rectangular base, six vertical strings, and a visible keyboard, blending classical and modern design elements.
description_view_0: The rear view highlights the lyre-shaped frame and the solid rectangular base, with the strings and upper arch clearly visible.
description_view_1: The left side view shows the depth of the base, the protruding rounded front leg, and the vertical alignment of the strings and column.
description_view_2: The front view reveals the keyboard beneath the strings, the lyre-shaped frame, and the two prominent rounded front legs.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the depth of the base, the keyboard, and the vertical structure of the column.
attribute_frame_design: The frame is lyre-shaped with two upwardly curved arms and a solid rectangular base, blending classical lyre and piano elements.
attribute_column_curvature: The column is gently curved, following the classic lyre silhouette with a smooth arch.
